summary: summarize(RawItem)->str returns a 1-2 sentence plain-text summary via LiteLLM
Cache hit returns cached summary without calling the LLM
Cache miss calls LLM once then set() stores result
Cache key = summary:{repo}:{item_type}:{item_id}:{content_hash}
content_hash = SHA-256 of submitted title+body; same content -> same key
LLM timeout caught, item skipped, run continues
LLM 4xx/5xx/429 degrade gracefully (skip+log+continue)
Failure logs never contain LLM key or secret prompt
Cache get failure (Redis down) treated as a miss; LLM still called
Cache set failure does not abort or lose the summary
Over-long LLM output normalized to <=2 sentences, not rejected
Fully empty item (no title+body) skipped without an LLM call
Over-long body truncated to bounded cap before LLM; hash over truncated text
Idempotent re-run over unchanged items makes zero new LLM calls
S4 makes no GitHub/State-Store calls; only LLM + cache (S2!=S4 boundary)
BR: a single item's LLM failure must never abort the whole run
BR: summary cache is best-effort; failure degrades, never crashes
BR: S4 sends data to no third party other than the single configured LLM provider
STRIDE triggered: issue text egress to third-party LLM provider (Information Disclosure, HIGH); mitigated by public-repo-only + single configured provider + README disclosure

decision: content_hash = SHA-256 hex of title + '\n' + body (post-truncation)
reasoning: newline separator prevents concatenation collisions; stable across runs; user confirmed at SPEC LOCK Q1

decision: LLM failure → SKIP item (no SummarizedItem emitted); no placeholder
reasoning: matches conventions.md 'skip-item, log, continue'; keeps S5 renderer free of special-case handling; user confirmed at SPEC LOCK Q2

decision: Input truncation cap = 8000 chars, hardcoded in V1
reasoning: covers most real GitHub issue bodies; ~2k tokens; config-driven is V2 concern; user confirmed at SPEC LOCK Q3

decision: No cache TTL in V1; content-hash is the sole invalidation mechanism
reasoning: content-hash handles edited-issue invalidation; TTL adds complexity with no V1 benefit; user confirmed at SPEC LOCK Q4

decision: No bespoke LLM retry/backoff in V1; rely on LiteLLM defaults; error → graceful skip
reasoning: PROJECT_SPEC §5 rate-limit handling is Collector's concern; retry loop adds latency with no guarantee; user confirmed at SPEC LOCK Q5

decision: RF-1 (data egress to LLM provider) acknowledged by user at SPEC LOCK
reasoning: public-repo-only + single configured provider + README disclosure accepted as sufficient V1 mitigation

decision: Cache-aside lives inside the concrete LiteLLMSummarizer adapter (cache injected at __init__); LLMClient Protocol unchanged
reasoning: AC-4-003 forbids Protocol change; mirrors GitHubCollector injected-client precedent; cache-aside is adapter concern not interface

decision: Single LLM error boundary: except litellm.exceptions.APIError (common base of Timeout/RateLimit/APIConnection/4xx/5xx)
reasoning: verified via MRO all litellm errors derive from APIError; precise catch covers AC-4-009/010 without swallowing programming bugs

decision: content_hash = sha256((title + '\n' + body).encode('utf-8')).hexdigest() over the truncated text
reasoning: SPEC-LOCK Q1; newline separator avoids concat collisions; UTF-8 safe; edit-sensitive (AC-4-008); hashing truncated text keeps key stable (AC-4-019)

decision: Best-effort cache via try/except Exception wrapper in adapter (_cache_get/_cache_set); RedisSummaryCache stays a thin faithful port that may raise
reasoning: AC-4-013/014 degrade-not-crash; keeps the port honest + testable; degradation policy in one place; broad except scoped to cache I/O only

decision: summarize(item)->str stays the pure Protocol method; add adapter-only summarize_items(items)->list[SummarizedItem] batch helper owning catch-log-skip-continue
reasoning: AC-4-003 keeps Protocol frozen; a ->str method cannot express skip (AC-4-011/018); mirrors state-store adapter-only is_seen/mark_seen
rejected: raise from summarize + catch in pipeline (spreads policy)

decision: normalize_summary: strip fences+whitespace, regex split on (?<=[.!?])\s+ with masked-abbrev guard, keep <=2 sentences; empty output raises SummarizationFailed (EC-012)
reasoning: A-A3 soft contract; stdlib re sufficient (search-first rejected NLP dep per over-engineering ban); empty must skip not cache

decision: Frozen SummarizerConfig(model, request_timeout_seconds=30.0, input_char_cap=8000, max_sentences=2, max_summary_chars=600); pass timeout= to litellm.completion
reasoning: lessons-learned config-driven-tunables; 30s bounds hung LLM (RF-2); 8000 cap enforces AC-4-019/RF-3; mirrors CollectorConfig

decision: Prompt = system(<=2 sentences plain text)+user(Title/Body); send only title+body to single configured provider; api_key passed to completion(api_key=) and held private, never a config field, never logged
reasoning: AC-4-001/002/022 + RF-1/RF-4; only title+body egress; key never in repr/logs (QA-testable)

decision: Tasks 1.1/1.2/2.1/2.2/2.3/2.4/3.1/3.2 complete. errors.py, config.py, keys.py, normalize.py, cache/redis_cache.py + 24 tests all passing. No deviations from design.
reasoning: Followed design.md §Implementation Guide order exactly. ADR-003 newline separator, ADR-006 masked-abbrev regex, ADR-004 faithful Redis port.

decision: LLM error boundary uses `except openai.APIError` instead of `except litellm.exceptions.APIError`
reasoning: MRO verified at runtime: litellm.exceptions.Timeout/RateLimitError/BadRequestError/InternalServerError all inherit from openai.APIError (not litellm.exceptions.APIError). issubclass(litellm.exceptions.Timeout, litellm.exceptions.APIError) == False. The design intent (catch all LLM errors precisely) is preserved; only the class reference changes.
rejected: litellm.exceptions.APIError (does not catch subclasses as expected at runtime)

decision: Tasks 4.1/5.1/5.2/5.3/5.4/5.5/6.1/6.2 complete. client.py + __init__.py exports + 45 tests, 164 total passing. Coverage 99.38%. All AC-4-001..022 covered. Security scan clean.
reasoning: Followed design.md Flow 1+2 pseudocode exactly. One deviation: openai.APIError catch (logged above).

summary: Shallow assertion in test_api_key_passed_to_completion_not_hardcoded_AC_4_022: `or completion.call_args[0]` branch makes assertion trivially true when completion is called with any positional args; api_key absence would not be caught
recommended_fix: Replace `assert kwargs.get('api_key') == 'secret-key-123' or completion.call_args[0]` with `assert kwargs.get('api_key') == 'secret-key-123'`

summary: .env.example has only 3 lines (gate requires >=10); new LLM env vars (LLM_PROVIDER, LLM_API_KEY) not documented as examples
rca: Pre-existing project gap; not introduced by this change
recommended_fix: Expand .env.example to >=10 lines covering all Config env vars with placeholder values in S6

summary: No README.md at project root (gate requires >=10 lines); RF-1 disclosure deferred to S6 by design
rca: Pre-existing project gap; S6 release task already captures this
recommended_fix: Create README.md in S6 with setup, config schema, and RF-1 privacy disclosure

decision: GO
reasoning: 0 Critical/High bugs; 22/22 ACs verified; 164/164 tests pass; coverage 99.38%; OWASP clean; all 5 watch items verified PASS; DEV-001 deviation confirmed correct; routes_to S6

decision: S6 release complete: BUG-002 (.env.example expanded to 33 lines) DONE, BUG-003 (README.md 159 lines with RF-1 disclosure) DONE, release.md written (migration N/A — no DB, rollback plan, 5-step smoke test, additive deploy strategy). 164/164 tests pass, 99.38% coverage, ruff clean. openspec archive pending.
reasoning: All three LOW bugs resolved. No migration needed (pure additive code drop, no DB/schema change). Pipeline.py not wired — feature is dormant until next change. Change archived via openspec archive.

decision: BUG-002 resolved: .env.example expanded from 3 lines to 33 lines covering GITHUB_TOKEN, LLM_PROVIDER, LLM_API_KEY, STATE_PATH (commented), REDIS_URL (commented) with full annotations and placeholder values only
reasoning: All new env vars introduced by the summarizer module are now documented. No real secrets included (R-SEC-001).

decision: BUG-003 resolved: README.md created at project root (159 lines) covering RF-1 privacy disclosure, requirements, setup, config schema (config.toml + .env), usage, key technical decisions, and development commands
reasoning: RF-1 requirement satisfied: README explicitly states only title+body are sent to the single configured LLM provider; no other fields egress.
